extraction.one_line_summary: PEDERSEN Mads won a chaotic flat sprint after UAE Emirates aggressively hunted intermediate sprint bonuses, with POGACAR taking 6 seconds at the sprint.
tactical_patterns: UAE Emirates sent POGACAR to contest flat intermediate sprint for bonuses 12.5km out, briefly taking virtual GC lead.
Bora-Hansgrohe attempted echelon split at 70.5km despite modest wind, showing proactive aggression ahead of crucial TTT stage.
Multiple sprinter leadouts fractured in final 500m due to traffic islands, exposing equipment choices favoring GC protection over sprint trains.

rider_signals.POGACAR Tadej: positive: contested flat intermediate sprint against pure sprinters MATTHEWS and HOOYDONCK, taking 6 bonus seconds to briefly claim virtual lead
rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: positive: won through center after multiple sprint trains disintegrated, first Danish Paris-Nice leader in 53 years before critical TTT
rider_signals.LAPORTE Pierre: negative: crashed in town section before wind picked up, required teammates to pace back, potentially compromising TTT form
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: negative: lost POPPEL's wheel inside final 500m at traffic islands, suggesting positioning or bike handling vulnerability
rider_signals.MERLIER Tim: negative: caught out before final 500m despite flat finish suiting pure sprinters, indicating team positioning failure
